scope.summary:
Goal: Track per-frame FRAME TIME on the UI thread and emit a typed `internal_diagnostics` event when a frame is SLOW (Master Spec v02.196 §5.8.2 'frame-time and resource counters'). Frame-time is the in-process degradation signal BELOW a full freeze: a frame that takes 250ms is not yet a 5s freeze, but it is a stutter the Diagnostics Panel (MT-087) should surface and the panel's frame-time stats (§5.8.4) need. This is the cheap in-process complement to Palmistry's external freeze detection.

Exact edit (build on MT-084's wire-in point):
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/app.rs` `fn update` — alongside the MT-084 heartbeat, measure the wall duration of the frame. Use a `last_frame_instant: Option<Instant>` field on HandshakeApp: at the top of `update`, compute `dt = now - last_frame_instant` (the inter-frame interval) and store `now`; this is the frame period. (Optionally also measure the WORK time inside update by sampling an Instant before `self.ui(ctx)` and after — the period is simplest + sufficient for the slow-frame flag; document which you use.)
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/diagnostics/frame_timing.rs` — a `FrameTimer` helper: rolling stats (last, min, max, and a small ring of the last ~120 frame durations for a p50/p95 the panel reads) + a `record_frame(dt: Duration)` that updates the stats and, when `dt` exceeds a SLOW threshold (default ~100ms => below ~10fps; make it a const, tune so normal 60fps frames never flag), calls `diagnostics::record_with(DiagEventCode::SlowFrame, DiagPhase::Tick, DiagSeverity::Warn, frame_micros, ...)`. Declare `pub mod frame_timing;` in diagnostics/mod.rs.
- Expose `pub fn frame_stats() -> FrameStats` (last/min/max/p50/p95 in micros) for the panel (MT-087). All typed integers (micros) — no content.
- DEBOUNCE the SlowFrame event so a sustained slow period does not flood the ring (e.g. emit at most one SlowFrame per ~1s window, or only on the rising edge into a slow streak + a count) — the ring is bounded (MT-081) and flooding it with SlowFrame would evict useful events. Use a simple last-emit Instant gate.
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_frame_timing.rs`.

Why the threshold matters (no false flags): the SlowFrame threshold (~100ms) sits ABOVE a normal 16ms (60fps) / 33ms (30fps) frame and the ~250ms idle-repaint cadence from MT-084 — BUT the idle 250ms repaint period would itself look like a slow frame if you measure the PERIOD. AVOID THIS: either measure WORK time inside update (before/after self.ui), OR exclude the idle path (only flag when the app was actively repainting due to input/animation, not the idle keep-alive). Document the chosen approach so the idle keep-alive heartbeat (MT-084) does not generate spurious SlowFrame spam. Measuring update WORK time (Instant around self.ui(ctx)) is the cleaner choice and is recommended.

Reuse note: builds directly on the MT-084 `fn update` edit + the MT-082 recorder. No backend. egui_kittest drives frames; a slow frame can be simulated by sleeping inside a test pane or by feeding a known duration to FrameTimer::record_frame directly (unit-level) plus a kittest that a real slow frame flags.

Risk if missed: no frame-time stats for the panel (§5.8.4 unmet); stutters below the freeze threshold are invisible; OR (if done wrong) the idle keep-alive is mis-flagged as slow, flooding the ring and burying real events.
acceptance_criteria: AC-005-1: a unit test feeds FrameTimer a sequence of durations including one above the slow threshold and asserts (a) the stats (last/min/max/p50/p95) are correct and (b) exactly one SlowFrame DiagEvent was recorded for the slow frame (typed micros, no content).

AC-005-2: a kittest drives the real HandshakeApp with an artificially slow frame (e.g. a test pane that sleeps past the threshold) and asserts a SlowFrame event reaches the ring/buffer from the live frame path — and that NORMAL fast frames do NOT flag.

AC-005-3: the idle keep-alive (MT-084 ~250ms repaint) does NOT produce spurious SlowFrame events — proven by stepping idle frames and asserting no SlowFrame is emitted (the chosen measurement excludes/handles the idle period; document the approach).

AC-005-4: SlowFrame is DEBOUNCED — a sustained slow streak emits a bounded number of events (not one per frame), proven by a test feeding many consecutive slow frames and asserting the emit count is gated (e.g. <= 1 per ~1s window).

AC-005-5: `frame_stats()` returns typed integer micros only (no content); the panel can read it. `cargo build -p handshake-native` clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; full suite `-j 2` green; external target dir; no repo-local target/.
proof_targets: PT-005-A: `cargo test -p handshake-native frame_timing` GREEN — stats correctness + one SlowFrame on the slow frame.
PT-005-B: kittest GREEN — real slow frame flags from the live path; fast frames do not.
PT-005-D: `cargo build --lib --bins` clean + clippy clean + full suite -j 2 green.
implementation_notes: Prefer measuring update WORK time (Instant before/after self.ui(ctx)) over the inter-frame PERIOD, so the MT-084 idle keep-alive (a long idle period with trivial work) is NOT mis-flagged as a slow frame. Document the choice.
Tune the slow threshold (~100ms) so 60fps (16ms) and 30fps (33ms) never flag; only genuine stutters do. Make it a named const for easy tuning.
Debounce: keep a last-slow-emit Instant; only emit a SlowFrame if > ~1s since the last, so a sustained slow period emits a bounded count (the ring is bounded — flooding it evicts useful events).
All exposed numbers are micros (u64) — typed allowlist; no content. p50/p95 over a small fixed ring (~120 samples) is enough for the panel.
Reuse the MT-084 `fn update` edit + MT-082 recorder; do not add a second frame timer or a second runtime.
Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; the slow-frame flag must fire from the REAL frame path (kittest), not only the unit feed.
ports_from_react: NONE (native frame timing; no React analogue)
binds_backend_api: NONE. Frame-time stats are in-process; SlowFrame is a local ring/buffer event. FR not involved.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: Frame-time stats + SlowFrame are Tier 2 in-process diagnostics; not Tier 1 business events, not forwarded to FR.
This MT IS the Tier 2 frame-time component (§5.8.2/§5.8.4): live frame-time tracking + a typed SlowFrame event + stats for the panel, wired into the live frame loop. Built + consumed here.

SlowFrame is an in-process stutter signal below a full freeze; Palmistry's external freeze detection (MT-091) reads the heartbeat staleness (MT-084), not the SlowFrame event. No Palmistry wiring is required for this MT.

risks: RISK-005-1: the MT-084 idle keep-alive (long idle period) is mis-measured as a slow frame, flooding the ring with false SlowFrame events and evicting real ones. Mitigate: measure update WORK time, not the period; AC-005-3.
RISK-005-2: a sustained slow streak emits one event per frame, flooding the bounded ring. Mitigate: debounce gate (AC-005-4).
RISK-005-3: a too-low threshold flags normal 30/60fps frames. Mitigate: tuned ~100ms const + AC-005-2 fast-frames-do-not-flag.
RISK-005-4: frame-time work adds latency to the very frame it measures. Mitigate: two Instant reads + an integer push are negligible; no alloc/format on the path.
minimum_controls: MC-005-1: idle path produces no spurious SlowFrame (AC-005-3).
MC-005-2: debounce bounds the emit rate (AC-005-4).
MC-005-3: real slow frame flags, fast frames do not (AC-005-2).
MC-005-4: external target dir + clippy -D warnings + full suite -j 2.

research_provenance.field_stack: Standard frame-time instrumentation (rolling p50/p95 over a small ring, slow-frame threshold + debounce) as used by game/GUI profilers; tuned so the egui idle keep-alive cadence is excluded.
research_provenance.motivating_incident: The 2026-06-26 freeze began as the UI thread stalling; frame-time tracking surfaces the degradation that precedes/accompanies a stall (slow frames before a full stop) as a panel-visible signal below the 5s freeze threshold.
research_provenance.validation_plan: stats correctness + live slow-frame flag + idle-no-false-flag + debounce; clippy -D warnings; full suite -j 2.

implementation_result.summary: FrameTimer/FrameStats (last/min/max/p50/p95 over 120-frame ring; ~100ms slow threshold; ~1s emit debounce) wired into the live update() measuring WORK-time around self.ui(ctx) -> record_frame_live -> debounced SlowFrame DiagEvent + frame_stats() accessor for MT-087. AC-005-1..5 PASS; idle keep-alive does NOT false-flag; typed micros. Pre-existing other-MT suite failures (file_save/quick_switcher/perf_lc07) baseline-proven unrelated.
implementation_result.notes: #[doc(hidden)] set_extra_frame_work_for_test seam (inert in production) for the live slow-frame proof.
